scope: ResourceKindPolicyV1 policy-shape and local resource unit/quantity decisions
source_basis: CONFORMANCE_MATRIX.json obligations ZRM-CBC-013 through ZRM-CBC-016 and ZRM-CBC-036 through ZRM-CBC-037
assumptions: ResourceKindPolicyV1 does not require its admitted resource set to be nonempty.
quantity_max is a general resource upper bound and is not normalized by accounting mode.
EvidenceOnly is not an explicit zero-quantity marker permission.
Schema version 1 is the only version supported by this oracle.
Reason precedence inside the ResourcePolicy stage is oracle-local unless the draft assigns a stable code.

normative_ambiguities.statement: The draft does not say that a policy must admit at least one resource.
normative_ambiguities.effect: A quantity_max of zero constructs but admits no resource under the v1 no-zero rule.
normative_ambiguities.required_resolution: Add an inhabited-policy invariant if disabling creation through an empty bound is forbidden.

normative_ambiguities.statement: The draft requires each LifecycleNonFungible resource to have quantity 1 but does not require LifecycleNonFungible quantity_max to equal 1.
normative_ambiguities.effect: Forcing quantity_max == 1 at policy construction is not derivable from the prior specification.
normative_ambiguities.required_resolution: An exact lifecycle-maximum equality requires an approved semantic amendment with compatibility and decision-precedence rules.

normative_ambiguities.statement: The v1 policy schema has no explicit permits_zero_quantity_marker field.
normative_ambiguities.effect: EvidenceOnly cannot safely serve as implicit zero-marker permission.
normative_ambiguities.required_resolution: Add a versioned explicit permission and define its interaction with accounting modes before admitting zero.

normative_ambiguities.statement: No quantity-only predicate distinguishes nonmonetary evidence from a fungible interpretation of the same positive integer.
normative_ambiguities.effect: Positive EvidenceOnly quantity acceptance is structural and cannot establish the required application meaning.
normative_ambiguities.required_resolution: Define mode-specific accounting/lifecycle semantics or add a policy commitment that makes the nonfungible evidence interpretation machine-checkable.

normative_ambiguities.statement: The draft fixes stage order but no stable ResourceKindPolicyV1 reason codes or complete within-stage precedence.
normative_ambiguities.effect: Schema-before-validity and unit-before-quantity select local oracle reasons only.
normative_ambiguities.required_resolution: Freeze reason codes and precedence before cross-language rejection parity is claimed.

findings.title: A general maximum check is insufficient for lifecycle quantity
findings.boundary: ResourcePolicy validation of a LifecycleNonFungible resource
attacker_capabilities: Choose untrusted resource quantity fields under a selected policy
Submit a quantity that is within the policy maximum
preconditions: The selected v1 policy is LifecycleNonFungible with unit unit-u and quantity_max 2
The resource uses unit unit-u and quantity 2
findings.invariant: Every admitted LifecycleNonFungible resource has quantity exactly 1.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For every valid lifecycle policy p and quantity q, acceptance implies q == 1 and q <= p.quantity_max.
findings.residual_risk: This local rule does not establish lifecycle authorization, uniqueness, or exact transition coverage.

findings.title: Lifecycle maximum zero is not forbidden by the prior policy schema
attacker_capabilities: Propose an inert policy candidate for governed review
preconditions: The v1 policy is LifecycleNonFungible with quantity_max 0
All other modeled fields are valid
findings.invariant: A reference interpreter does not strengthen policy-construction rules beyond the normative specification.

expected_result.follow_up: No resource quantity is admitted because zero lacks explicit permission and one exceeds the maximum.

findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Policy construction accepts every u128 quantity_max unless a separate normative mode-coherence rule forbids it.
findings.residual_risk: If empty policies are unintended, the draft needs an inhabited-policy rule; adding it changes specified behavior.

findings.title: Lifecycle maximum above one does not relax the exact resource quantity
findings.boundary: Composition of policy construction and ResourcePolicy validation
attacker_capabilities: Propose an inert lifecycle policy candidate
Submit a resource under that selected policy
preconditions: The v1 policy is LifecycleNonFungible with quantity_max 2
The resource quantity and unit are exactly 1 and unit-u
findings.invariant: The exact lifecycle-resource rule and the general maximum rule are independent conjuncts.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For lifecycle p with p.quantity_max >= 1, quantity 1 is structurally admissible regardless of how much larger the maximum is.
findings.residual_risk: Requiring quantity_max == 1 may be a desirable canonicalization rule, but it needs an approved semantic amendment and migration decision.

findings.title: EvidenceOnly is not implicit zero-marker permission
findings.boundary: ResourcePolicy validation of an EvidenceOnly resource
attacker_capabilities: Select EvidenceOnly in an inert policy candidate
preconditions: The v1 policy has EvidenceOnly mode and quantity_max 1
The resource has the matching unit and quantity 0
findings.invariant: Zero quantity is admitted only when resource-kind policy explicitly permits a zero-quantity marker.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Until a versioned explicit permission exists, acceptance implies quantity_atoms > 0 for every accounting mode.
findings.residual_risk: The schema cannot presently express the specification's intended zero-marker exception.

findings.title: Positive EvidenceOnly quantity has observationally ambiguous meaning
findings.boundary: Application interpretation after structural ResourcePolicy validation
attacker_capabilities: Choose application semantics for a positive EvidenceOnly quantity
Present the same policy and resource fields under a fungible interpretation
preconditions: The v1 policy has EvidenceOnly mode, matching unit, and quantity_max 1
findings.invariant: EvidenceOnly resources do not carry monetary or fungible quantity semantics.

expected_result.non_claim: nonmonetary and nonfungible semantics remain unestablished

findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Either define a machine-checkable evidence interpretation or prove that transition and application policies cannot use EvidenceOnly quantity fungibly.
findings.residual_risk: A quantity-only oracle cannot distinguish the two interpretations, so structural acceptance must not be promoted into a semantic compliance claim.

findings.title: Equal atoms with different units remain incomparable
attacker_capabilities: Choose a resource unit distinct from the selected policy unit
preconditions: The policy unit is unit-u and maximum is 1
The resource unit is unit-v and quantity is 1
findings.invariant: A resource unit equals its selected resource-kind policy unit.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Resource admission implies exact UnitId equality; conversion belongs to an explicit transformation rule.
findings.residual_risk: This check does not define conversion ratios, rounding, or transformation authority.

findings.title: The u128 maximum is accepted without narrowing
findings.boundary: Resource quantity representation and maximum comparison
attacker_capabilities: Submit the largest representable resource quantity
preconditions: The policy maximum and resource quantity are both 2^128 - 1
The policy mode is AuthorityMintableFungible and units match
findings.invariant: Every representable u128 boundary value is preserved exactly and compared without truncation.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: The decision at u128::MAX equals the mathematical bounded-integer decision and performs no narrowing conversion.
findings.residual_risk: Transition sums still require checked arithmetic and may overflow even when each individual quantity is representable.

findings.title: A value above u128 must reject instead of wrapping
findings.boundary: Untrusted numeric input before protocol quantity interpretation
attacker_capabilities: Supply a host-language integer larger than the protocol width
findings.invariant: Values outside u128 are rejected and never narrowed or wrapped.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For every host integer q outside [0, 2^128 - 1], resource quantity admission rejects before semantic comparison.
findings.residual_risk: A strict wire decoder should make this host-language case unreachable; adapters still need differential evidence.
